records.ety: [L. excellere, excelsum; ex out + a root found in culmen height, top; cf. F. exceller. See Culminate, Column.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Excelled(?); p. pr. & vb. n. Excelling.]

defs: To go beyond or surpass in good qualities or laudable deeds; to outdo or outgo, in a good sense.
quotes: Excelling others, these were great; Thou, greater still, must these excel.
I saw that wisdom excelleth folly, as far as light excelleth darkness.

defs: To exceed or go beyond; to surpass.
quotes: She opened; but to shut Excelled her power; the gates wide open stood.

defs: To surpass others in good qualities, laudable actions, or acquirements; to be distinguished by superiority; as, to excel in mathematics, or classics.
quotes: Unstable as water, thou shalt not excel.
Then peers grew proud in horsemanship t' excel.
